name: qmd
tagline: ClawHub (108★): Local search/indexing CLI (BM25 + vectors + rerank) with MCP mode.
verdict: If the qmd binary is missing or the Ollama service is unreachable, the skill gives no instructions for what to do next — it can stall or fail confusingly mid-task. It also never says what happens if you re-run indexing on the same name/path, risking duplicate or corrupted indexes, and never states what it's not for.

highlights.summary: The two failure-prone dependencies (the qmd binary and the Ollama service) are both mentioned but nowhere does the skill say what to do if qmd is missing or Ollama is unreachable/n
No mention of what happens if this (or `qmd update`) is run a second time with the same name/path — no existence check or idempotency note is given for the mutating indexing steps.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in description or body; skill has multiple subcommands (index, search variants, MCP) so misfire against adjacent
